2604.03131.title: A Systematic Security Evaluation of OpenClaw and Its Variants
2604.03131.paper_summary: Tool-augmented AI agents substantially extend the practical capabilities of large language models, but they also introduce security risks that cannot be identified through model-only evaluation. In this paper, we present a systematic security assessment of six representative OpenClaw-series agent frameworks, namely OpenClaw, AutoClaw, QClaw, KimiClaw, MaxClaw, and ArkClaw, under multiple backbone models. To support this study, we construct a benchmark of 205 test cases covering representative attack behaviors across the full agent execution lifecycle, enabling unified evaluation of risk exposure at both the framework and model levels. Our results show that all evaluated agents exhibit substantial security vulnerabilities, and that agentized systems are significantly riskier than their underlying models used in isolation. In particular, reconnaissance and discovery behaviors emerge as the most common weaknesses, while different frameworks expose distinct high-risk profiles, including credential leakage, lateral movement, privilege escalation, and resource development. These findings indicate that the security of modern agent systems is shaped not only by the safety properties of the backbone model, but also by the coupling among model capability, tool use, multi-step planning, and runtime orchestration. We further show that once an agent is granted execution capability and persistent runtime context, weaknesses arising in early stages can be amplified into concrete system-level failures. Overall, our study highlights the need to move beyond prompt-level safeguards toward lifecycle-wide security governance for intelligent agent frameworks.
2604.03131.paper_summary_zh: 工具增強的人工智慧代理顯著擴展了大型語言模型的實際能力，但它們也引入了無法通過僅模型評估識別的安全風險。在本文中，我們對六個代表性的 OpenClaw 系列代理框架進行了系統的安全評估，這些框架分別是 OpenClaw、AutoClaw、QClaw、KimiClaw、MaxClaw 和 ArkClaw，並在多個主幹模型下進行評估。為了支持這項研究，我們構建了一個包含 205 個測試案例的基準，涵蓋了整個代理執行生命周期中的代表性攻擊行為，使得在框架和模型層面上能夠統一評估風險暴露。我們的結果顯示，所有評估的代理都表現出顯著的安全漏洞，而代理化系統的風險顯著高於單獨使用的基礎模型。特別是，偵察和發現行為成為最常見的弱點，而不同的框架則暴露出不同的高風險特徵，包括憑證洩漏、橫向移動、特權提升和資源開發。這些發現表明，現代代理系統的安全性不僅受到主幹模型安全性質的影響，還受到模型能力、工具使用、多步規劃和運行時編排之間耦合的影響。我們進一步顯示，一旦代理被授予執行能力和持久運行時上下文，早期階段出現的弱點可能會放大為具體的系統級故障。總體而言，我們的研究強調了需要超越提示級別的保障，朝向智能代理框架的全生命周期安全治理。
2604.03131.author: Yuhang Wang et.al.
2604.03131.authors: Yuhang Wang, Haichang Gao, Zhenxing Niu, Zhaoxiang Liu, Wenjing Zhang, Xiang Wang, Shiguo Lian
